business.headline: Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude stays low, FII mood improvesThe BSE Sensex was up 48.58 points, or 0.06%, at 77,149.05 at around 9:20 am, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 44.65 points, or 0.19%, to trade at 24,100.65 as of 9:23 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets rise or fall after a 3-day break?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,093 around 8:07 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open close to Friday's closing level of 24,056, suggesting a muted start to the trading session.

Gold prices fall: Indians sell old jewellery as fears of deeper correction growAs gold prices retreat from record highs, Indian households are selling old jewellery to lock in gains. The trend is lifting organised recycling volumes and may help reduce reliance on imports.

India reclaims 5th spot among world's biggest stock markets after Asian selloffIndia has regained the world's fifth-largest stock market position after Taiwan and South Korea slipped. The shift highlights how easing crude prices, foreign buying and regional corrections are reshaping rankings.

Kotak Mahindra Bank MD and CEO Ashok Vaswani to step down at year-endKotak Mahindra Bank has begun searching for a successor after CEO Ashok Vaswani said he will not seek reappointment when his term ends in December 2026. The move comes as the lender seeks faster growth after regulatory scrutiny and may keep investors focused on leadership continuity.

NSE, Jio IPOs have retail investors excited. But are big listings worth the hype?NSE and Jio are already generating plenty of excitement. But before investors join the rush, recent IPO history offers an important lesson about hype, valuations and long-term returns. Read on to find out why big listings don't always translate into big gains.

Will life insurance pay after a fire? Here's what every family should knowRecent fire tragedies have triggered questions over whether life insurance pays after accidental deaths. Experts say standard term plans generally cover such deaths unless false disclosures or policy exclusions apply.

Asian markets crash, will Dalal Street feel the aftershock on Monday?From South Korea's Kospi to Japan's Nikkei, Asian stock markets have tumbled sharply, leaving investors on edge. As Dalal Street prepares to reopen after a three-day holiday on Monday, will the BSE and NSE also open deep in the red?

From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

Govt restores commercial LPG supply to pre-West Asia crisis levelsThe move comes after the government reviewed the country's fuel availability and found that both domestic production and imported LPG supplies have improved.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

entertainment.headline: Explained: Farrhana Bhatt's family row, alleged sister's claims, mother's response Farrhana Bhatt's mother Afroza has denied Faizana's allegations in the public dispute involving Farrhana Bhatt and their family. Her video has intensified online reactions as Farrhana still has not issued a response.

Saif Ali Khan on raising sons with Kareena beyond religion: One God, many names Actor Saif Ali Khan opened up about the conversations he has with his sons, Taimur and Jehangir, about religion. He reflected on his interfaith upbringing and the values he hopes to pass on to his family.

In the name of Rama: Fresh Ramayana spins face box office test Ramayana, Varanasi and Alpha aim to recast elements of the epic. But recent experiments with the epic have strived to find takers among a deeply reverential audience.

Maa Inti Bangaaram beats Arundhati: Samantha scores Telugu's top woman-led hit Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram has overtaken Arundhati's lifetime worldwide total on Day 10. The milestone makes it Telugu cinema's biggest woman-led hit while extending its strong theatrical run.

Alia Bhatt, Sharvari tease Alpha track Massacre. Fans demand full song release A short preview of Alpha song Massacre from actors Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Instagram Live has caught fans' attention online. The catchy track has sparked calls for Yash Raj Films to release it immediately.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 3: Akshay's film nears Rs 100 crore globally Actor Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle continued its strong box office run over the weekend. The comedy film has now moved close to the Rs 100-crore mark worldwide.

Vir Das rubbishes Revolver Rani kiss injury claim against Kangana: Pure fiction Comedian Vir Das on Sunday rejected claims that a kissing scene with actor Kangana Ranaut in Revolver Rani left him injured. His response revives an old rumour and countered what he called unfair slander against her.

Rakhi Sawant calls Akanksha 'Zuthi' after she confirms divorce from Gaurav Khanna Rakhi Sawant's reaction to Akanksha Chamola's divorce announcement on Lock Upp 2 has sparked fresh online debate. Here's what happened and why the comment is grabbing attention.

Actor Jasmine Bhasin hospitalised in Dubai, partner Aly Goni shares update Actor Jasmine Bhasin was hospitalised in Dubai after falling seriously ill during her birthday trip with Aly Goni. As concern grew among fans and friends, Aly said she is slowly recovering.

JLo performs On The Floor at Obsessed Fest and introduces the Off Campus cast Jennifer Lopez surprised fans at Prime Video's Obsessed Fest with a live performance and a special introduction for the Off Campus cast. Here's why the moment has everyone talking.

Anirudh Ravichander-Kavya Maran wedding on the cards? Uncle says it's confirmed Wedding rumours surrounding music composer Anirudh Ravichander and his partner Kavya Maran have resurfaced after a family member made a striking claim.

Bollywood trade union rallies behind Satendra Soni, seeks probe into Ped Palki row Bollywood trade union AICWA has backed actor Satendra Soni and sought an FIR against the makers of Ped Palki after his allegations of unpaid dues and intimidation. Here's what the actor claimed and why the industry body has stepped in.

Zaid Darbar admits he was nervous before meeting Gauahar Khan's ex Kushal Zaid Darbar admits he was anxious before coming face-to-face with wife Gauahar Khan's former boyfriend Kushal Tandon on Alliance. Here's what happened when the two finally met.

Anil Kapoor pays tribute to K Bhagyaraj, says his stories shaped his career Anil Kapoor remembered K Bhagyaraj with an emotional note after the filmmaker's death, revealing how the late writer's stories shaped some of the biggest milestones in his career.

Never miss my target: Ssunita hints at 'third party' in Govinda shooting incident Ssunita Ahuja made a cryptic remark about Govinda's accidental shooting incident, hinting at a "third party" in his life. She also claimed that the actor had "many affairs" during their marriage while reflecting on their relationship.

Did Kriti Sanon and Kabir Bahia call it quits?Here's the truth behind viral photo A viral photograph of Kabir Bahia with another woman sparked fresh speculation about his rumoured relationship with Kriti Sanon. But is there any truth to the breakup rumours? Here's what a source has revealed.

Sharvari, Vedang Raina watch Main Vaapas Aaunga in theatre, thank fans in a note Actor Sharvari shared a heartfelt note after watching Main Vaapas Aaunga with co-star Vedang Raina in a theatre. The actor said the love for Jiya and Keenu has helped the Imtiaz Ali directorial reach more hearts with each passing week.

Friends star Courteney Cox, Johnny McDaid split after 11 years? Here's what we know Friends star Courteney Cox and Snow Patrol musician Johnny McDaid have reportedly ended their 11-year relationship. According to a report, the split was amicable, with the couple remaining close friends despite deciding to go their separate ways.

Actor Harshad Chopda recalls being cheated on by his girlfriend with his best friend Television actor Harshad Chopda made an emotional confession on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa, revealing that his girlfriend cheated on him with his best friend. The actor said the betrayal, which happened in 2010, left him unable to trust people and that he had kept the pain to himself for 16 years.

general news.headline: If you were at Pune fort on day of Ketan's murder, come forward: Father's appealKetan Agarwal's family demanded death penalty for the accused and appealed to eyewitnesses to assist the investigation, as police questioned Siya Goyal's parents and continued gathering evidence in the Lohagad Fort murder case.

Will Mojtaba Khamenei publicly surface for the first time at his father's funeral?Iran is preparing for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's state funeral. The current attention now is whether Ayatollah's son, the present Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, makes his first public appearance. Mojtaba's continued absence has been increasing questions and speculation over his health, authority, and the leadership transition.

Delayed or denied? Sooryavanshi snub splits opinion after Ireland shockerVaibhav Sooryavanshi was benched throughout India's series defeat to Ireland, splitting opinion among former players. Gavaskar, Kaif and Tiwary back his inclusion. Ashwin backs the management. The debate now shadows India's England tour from July 1.

Fight with brother, tea break: Cab driver reveals Siya's actions before Bali tripThe cab driver claimed that Siya Goyal stopped the cab midway on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway, asked him to open the boot, and took an unidentified item from her purse before the group reached Mumbai Airport.

IndiGo landed a jet via Gagan, not radio. What is it, and why does it matter for India?On June 27, 2026, an IndiGo A320 became the first jet in India to land using Gagan, the country's own satellite navigation system. Here is how this technology guides aircraft to the runway without any ground-based radio equipment.

Ford rehiring old engineers after AI failed at workFord thought artificial intelligence could solve its quality problems, but the technology fell short without decades of engineering know-how. The automaker has now brought back hundreds of veteran engineers to train both younger employees and its AI, and the strategy is already paying off.

Pakistan carries out strikes near Afghan border after Karachi attack, 29 deadPakistan mounted a ground operation in Bajaur and strikes across the Afghan frontier, killing 29 people. The action followed an attempted attack on the Karachi headquarters of Pakistan Rangers earlier in the weekend.

Video: Missed exit turns deadly, reversing car hit by SUV, 4 family members dieA CCTV video shows a reversing car being hit by a speeding Scorpio on the Delhi-Dehradun corridor in Saharanpur. The footage is now central to the police probe into the crash that killed four family members.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 3: Akshay's film nears Rs 100 crore globallyActor Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle continued its strong box office run over the weekend. The comedy film has now moved close to the Rs 100-crore mark worldwide.

Delhi records warmest morning in 2 years as monsoon delay leaves city swelteringDelhi's minimum temperature touched 31.1 degrees Celsius, the highest in two years, as delayed monsoon winds kept the capital unusually hot and humid with heatwave conditions.

Not 2 but 11 marks gained: CBSE says Vedant's claim is a blatant lieVedant Srivastava has received his CBSE re-evaluation result following the Physics answer-sheet mix-up. However, his claims have been challenged by CBSE, which said he received an 11-mark increase, not two, and called his statement "factually incorrect."

Gold prices fall: Indians sell old jewellery as fears of deeper correction growAs gold prices retreat from record highs, Indian households are selling old jewellery to lock in gains. The trend is lifting organised recycling volumes and may help reduce reliance on imports.

India makes hydrogen from nuclear heat, not electricity. Why scientists are thrilledIndia has inaugurated the world's first plant that makes hydrogen using nuclear reactor heat instead of electricity. Built at Kalpakkam, the copper-chlorine cycle points to a cleaner, round-the-clock route to green hydrogen.

Bengal likely to table UCC Bill today; BJP, divided TMC brace for first showdownThe UCC debate is set to become the first major political confrontation between the BJP government and both TMC factions, with the ruling party defending a key manifesto promise and the opposition preparing a coordinated challenge inside and outside the Assembly.

Heavy rain returns to Mumbai, Andheri subway shut amid waterloggingHeavy rain returned to Mumbai after a brief weekend lull, prompting fresh weather alerts as forecasters warned of thunderstorms, gusty winds and possible flooding. Wet conditions are expected to persist across the city and neighbouring regions through the week.

US, Iran agree to halt strikes, hold talks this week in Doha: ReportThe US and Iran have agreed to suspend all 'kinetic activity' while technical negotiations continue over the Strait of Hormuz, according to Axios.

Has Delhi's Sarojini Nagar lost its charm? Here's what we saw on a SundayThe short answer is no - Sarojini Nagar has not lost its charm despite what several Instagram videos suggest. Read on to know why we say so after a Sunday visit to the market.

After CBSE tender probe, Sarthak Siddant launches 1.66 crore govt record portalClass 12 student Sarthak Sidhant has launched a public portal carrying around 1.66 crore procurement records from the CPP Portal. Sarthak says the database is meant to help citizens, journalists and researchers examine government contracts more easily.

2,004 calls, a missing phone, no internet for 11 hours: How cops traced ChetanOfficials said that Chetan travelled to Lohagad Fort carrying an employee's phone instead of his own to avoid digital tracking. To verify his whereabouts, police contacted people who had called his number that day.

Why men looking for sex reach Congress House in MumbaiMumbai's Congress House, the pre-Independence-era landmark, is not a brothel, but men driven by lust and desire often fail to make the distinction. The former Congress office's proximity to a mujra house and the red-light district of Kamathipura make it carry a stigma. However, it is not really untouched by the sex trade.

In the name of Rama: Fresh Ramayana spins face box office testRamayana, Varanasi and Alpha aim to recast elements of the epic. But recent experiments with the epic have strived to find takers among a deeply reverential audience.

Canada end brave Bafana Bafana dream with stoppage-time heartbreakStephen Eustaquio scored deep in stoppage time as Canada beat South Africa 1-0 in Los Angeles. The defeat ended Bafana Bafana's brave World Cup run, while Canada reached the Round of 16 for the first time.

Severe heatwave grips Europe; over 1,300 deaths, wildfires rage across continentScientists have described the ongoing heatwave as the worst ever in Europe, with extreme temperatures straining healthcare systems, disrupting power generation and damaging critical infrastructure across several countries. Forecasters, however, expect cooler conditions to return to much of Western Europe later this week.

Monsoon missing, but revival soon: These states on cusp of getting rainAfter a largely dry June that left India well short of normal rain, the monsoon is reviving as a low-pressure trough and several wind circulations strengthen. Here is which states get rain first, why the Northeast keeps flooding, and when Delhi can expect relief.

sports.headline: Cape Verde captain under investigation for alleged rape ahead of Argentina clash Cape Verde's historic FIFA World Cup run has been overshadowed by serious allegations. Brazilian publication Globo is reporting that the team's captain, Ryan Mendes, is under investigation in New Zealand over an alleged sexual assault during the FIFA Series in March.

India women self-destruct, have no one else to blame for T20 World Cup exit Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India crashed out of the tournament after a six-wicket defeat to Australia at Lord's on Sunday. The loss highlighted familiar shortcomings in bowling, fielding and decision-making, exposing the areas India must address if they are to consistently challenge the world's best.

Delayed or denied? Sooryavanshi snub splits opinion after Ireland shocker Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was benched throughout India's series defeat to Ireland, splitting opinion among former players. Gavaskar, Kaif and Tiwary back his inclusion. Ashwin backs the management. The debate now shadows India's England tour from July 1.

Canada end brave Bafana Bafana dream with stoppage-time heartbreak Stephen Eustaquio scored deep in stoppage time as Canada beat South Africa 1-0 in Los Angeles. The defeat ended Bafana Bafana's brave World Cup run, while Canada reached the Round of 16 for the first time.

Serena Williams reveals why she accepted Wimbledon singles comeback Serena Williams admits she nearly walked away from one final Wimbledon singles opportunity before convincing herself to embrace the moment. The 23-time Grand Slam champion will return to Centre Court after almost four years away, insisting she could not let the chance slip by.

Lack joy and happiness: Quaresma explains Ronaldo-led Portugal's struggles FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal scraped into the knockout stage after a flat goalless draw with Colombia. The stale display has intensified scrutiny of Cristiano Ronaldo, midfield control and the side's wider cohesion issues.

South Korea coach resigns after World Cup exit as President Lee lashes out FIFA World Cup 2026: Hong Myung-bo resigned after South Korea crashed out in the World Cup group stage. The exit provoked President Lee's attack on football administration and renewed scrutiny of the appointment process.

Harmanpreet to continue as India captain? India coach Amol Muzumdar opens up Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: India head coach Amol Muzumdar addressed questions over Harmanpreet Kaur's future as captain following India's early exit from the Women's T20 World Cup.

Retiring Ben Stokes opens batting, swings without fear as England bids farewell ENG vs NZ: Ben Stokes' final innings for England lasted just 20 balls, but it was filled with everything that defined his career. After announcing his shock retirement, the England captain signed off with a fearless 30, a standing ovation and a guard of honour from New Zealand.

RSA 0-1 CAN Highlights: Canada advance to Round of 16 with late win South Africa (RSA) vs Canada (CAN), FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: Canada left it painfully late, but Stephen Eustaquio's stoppage-time strike finally broke South African resistance to send Jesse Marsch's side into the Round of 16 with a 1-0 win over South Africa in LA.

India not good enough against top teams: Harmanpreet Kaur after early exit Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur admitted India need to raise their standards against the top teams and rethink their approach after their semifinal hopes ended with a defeat to Australia.

T20 world champions India lose series 0-2 in Ireland as 16-series streak endsIreland defended 154 and beat India by one run in Belfast to complete a 2-0 T20I series sweep. The result ended India's 16-series bilateral winning streak and exposed a brittle top order.

ODI champs India suffer Women's T20 World Cup heartbreak, crash out before semisWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India failed to advance to the semis after losing to Australia by six wickets in their first-ever T20I at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground. From Group A, South Africa and Australia went through to the semis.

Rohit Yadav hurls world's second-best javelin throw of 2026, seals Asian Games berthRohit Yadav produced a career-best 87.05m throw to win gold at the Interstate Athletics Championships, qualify for the 2026 Asian Games and register the second-best javelin throw in the world this season.

Virat Kohli cheers for Harmanpreet's record Lord's fifty, attends game with AnushkaVirat Kohli, Shikhar Dhawan, Anushka Sharma, Sophie Shine and Ravi Shastri were among the notable personalities at Lord's as India faced Australia in a crucial Women's T20 World Cup clash on Sunday.

Expected him to play both games: Sunil Gavaskar surprised at Sooryavanshi's Ireland omissionIND vs IRE: Sunil Gavaskar said he was surprised that Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was not given a debut in the Ireland T20I series despite his strong IPL performances. However, he believes the teenager remains in India's plans and is likely to get his opportunity during the upcoming England white-ball series.

Formula 1: George Russell wins in Austria, Verstappen and Antonelli join him on podiumFormula 1: George Russell won the Austrian Grand Prix from pole despite suffering a drinks system failure and extreme heat conditions, holding off Max Verstappen and Kimi Antonelli. The result trimmed Antonelli's championship lead and extended Mercedes' strong run in both the drivers' and constructors' standings.

Ben Stokes announces shock retirement from international midway during a TestEngland captain Ben Stokes has announced his retirement from international cricket and will draw the curtains down on his career after the ongoing Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge. A World Cup winner, Ashes hero and architect of England's Bazball revolution, Stokes leaves behind one of the greatest legacies in modern English cricket.

South Africa beat Bangladesh: What India now need to reach Women's T20 World Cup semisWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs AUS: After South Africa's four-wicket win over Bangladesh, what do India need to do against Australia at Lord's to secure a place in the semifinals?

Hopefully, I'll meet him: Novak Djokovic eyes Virat Kohli meet on India visitNovak Djokovic says he plans to visit India and finally meet Virat Kohli. The long-distance sporting friendship now edges towards a public moment as Wimbledon opens and Djokovic chases history.

No Sooryavanshi debut in Ireland, Suryansh and Prince get India T20I capsIRE vs IND: India brought in Suryansh Shedge and Prince Yadav for the second T20I in Belfast. The changes followed Ireland's landmark win, while Vaibhav Sooryavanshi remained on the bench.

IND vs AUS Highlights: India crash out after 7-wicket loss to AustraliaIndia (IND-W) vs Australia (AUS-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: India crashed out of the tournament after losing to Australia by six wickets on Sunday at the Lord's Cricket Ground. Chasing 171, the Aussies romped home with five balls to spare.

2nd T20I Highlights: India's 16 series unbeaten streak ends, Ireland win 2-0India (IND) vs Ireland (IRE), 2nd T20I Highlights: Ireland pulled off a famous one-run win to stun the T20 world champions in Belfast. The victory ended India's 16-series unbeaten run and handed them their first bilateral T20I series defeat since 2023.

World Cup fallout: Uruguay scraps team charter flight, players fly commercialUruguay's disappointing FIFA World Cup campaign appears to have ended in bitter fashion. Reports suggest that the football federation of the country has cancelled the team's charter flight and asked the players and the staff to fly commercial.

Sport has never had more greatness. Have we forgotten how to watch?Handcrafted ByOur Editors From Messi and Ronaldo to Federer, Nadal and Djokovic, modern sport has handed us an embarrassment of riches. Instead of enjoying it, we keep reaching for the scoreboard. Why has the GOAT debate become a habit we can't seem to break?

2 Indian army men script history, win first-ever gold in World Rowing CupIndia created history at the 2026 World Rowing Cup III in Lucerne after Lakshay and Ujjwal Kumar Singh won the country's first-ever gold medal at the event. The Indian Army rowers topped the Lightweight Men's Double Sculls final to secure a landmark result for Indian rowing.

Haaland drops cheeky message as Argentina maintain unbeaten run in World CupErling Haaland has dropped a cheeky message after Lionel Messi's record-extending 19th goal in the FIFA World Cup. Haaland's hilarious message has gone viral on the internet.

Honour above victory: Iran bid goodbye to FIFA World Cup with emotional noteIran's hopes of reaching the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time ended in heartbreak after Austria's last-gasp equaliser against Algeria sealed their exit. But instead of focusing on elimination, Iran signed off with an emotional message on honour, fairness and standing tall despite missing out.

Novak Djokovic eyes Wimbledon history with Serena Williams as inspirationNovak Djokovic hailed Serena Williams' Wimbledon comeback as "inspirational and epic", praising the American great's determination to return at 44 as he chases history at the All England Club.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
